title: 10 best herbal decoctions for urinary calculus
intro_desc: Herbal decoctions for urinary calculus are concentrated liquid extracts made from a combination of herbs, which have been traditionally used to treat kidney stones and other urinary tract issues. These decoctions help to dissolve and flush out stones by increasing urine flow, reducing inflammation, and breaking down mineral deposits. For example, the decoction of stone root (Collinsonia canadensis) and goldenrod (Solidago virgaurea) has been found to be effective in treating kidney stones, as it helps to increase urine output and reduce pain and discomfort. This improves lives by allowing individuals to manage their symptoms more effectively, reducing the risk of complications, and improving overall quality of life.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion decoctions helps with urinary calculus because of its natural diuretic properties, which stimulate the kidneys to increase urine production and flush out small stones. The decoction's high water content also helps to dilute the concentration of minerals in the urine, making it more difficult for them to precipitate and form stones. Additionally, dandelion's anti-inflammatory and antioxidant compounds may help reduce inflammation and prevent further stone formation. By promoting healthy kidney function and reducing stone formation, dandelion decoctions can provide relief from urinary calculus symptoms.
remedy_properties: Diuretic property: Dandelion decoction helps to increase urine production, which can help flush out small stones and gravel from the kidneys and bladder, reducing the risk of further stone formation.

Anti-inflammatory property: The anti-inflammatory compounds in dandelion may help reduce swelling and pain associated with urinary calculus, making it a valuable natural remedy for alleviating symptoms like burning urination and frequent urination.

Alkalizing property: Dandelion decoction has an alkaline effect on the urine, which can help neutralize the acidic environment that fosters stone formation. This may help prevent further calcification of minerals in the kidneys and bladder.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the dandelion plant have been traditionally used to treat urinary tract infections and stones, as they contain antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that can help reduce inflammation and promote healing in the urinary tract.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes of the dandelion plant are used due to their diuretic properties, which can help increase urine production and flush out small calculi or stones from the kidneys.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality taraxacum officinale leaves and flowers, ensuring freshness for optimal potency.
Rinse the plant material gently with cold running water to remove any dirt or debris.
Combine one tablespoon of dried taraxacum officinale herb with two cups of boiling distilled water in a heat-resistant glass container.
Allow the mixture to simmer for 10-15 minutes, reducing the liquid to approximately one cup, before straining and discarding solids.
Consume one quarter cup of the warm decoction three times daily, as needed, up to eight weeks for optimal results.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tree turmeric decoctions helps with urinary calculus because it has potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the urinary tract. The curcuminoids present in turmeric have been shown to inhibit the growth of bacteria that can contribute to kidney stone formation, while also helping to break down and eliminate calcium oxalate stones. Additionally, turmeric's natural antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties may help to reduce the risk of UTIs, which can increase the likelihood of developing urinary calculi.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Turmeric decoctions have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce inflammation and swelling associated with kidney stones, making it easier to pass the stones.
Antioxidant property: Antioxidants present in turmeric decoctions help neutralize free radicals that can damage kidneys and increase oxidative stress, which can exacerbate kidney stone formation.
Uric acid-lowering property: Turmeric has been shown to decrease uric acid levels in the body, which can help prevent the formation of new kidney stones or reduce the size of existing ones.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rich in curcumin, a powerful anti-inflammatory compound that helps alleviate pain and discomfort associated with urinary tract stones.
Roots: Known for their antioxidant properties, which help prevent oxidative stress and inflammation in the kidneys and urinary tract.
remedy_recipe: Measure 20 grams of dried berberis aristata root and place it in a heat-resistant container.
Add 500 milliliters of boiling water to the container and let it steep for 30 minutes.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into another container.
Discard the solids and transfer the decoction to a clean glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid.
Store the decoction in the refrigerator and consume within 24 hours.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Burdock decoctions helps with urinary calculus because its diuretic properties increase urine production, which helps to flush out small stones and gravel from the urinary tract. The antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds in burdock also reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the kidneys and ureters, making it an effective remedy for preventing stone formation. Additionally, burdock's mucilaginous properties soothe and protect the urinary tract lining, reducing irritation and promoting healthy kidney function.
remedy_properties: Diuretic property: Burdock root increases urine production, which helps to flush out kidney stones and reduce their size by increasing fluid flow through the kidneys and urinary tract.

Anti-inflammatory property: Burdock's anti-inflammatory compounds can help reduce swelling and pain in the kidneys and urinary tract associated with stone formation, making it a soothing remedy for kidney stone sufferers.

Antioxidant property: Burdock's rich antioxidant content helps protect the kidneys and urinary tract from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to stone formation and exacerbate existing conditions.
remedy_parts: Roots: Burdock roots are used to make decoctions that help alleviate symptoms of urinary calculi, such as pain and inflammation, due to their anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzome is used in decoctions to dissolve and flush out kidney stones, as it has diuretic and lithotrophic properties that help break down stone formation.
remedy_recipe: Collect fresh or dried arctium lappa roots in the amount of 20-30 grams.
Clean the roots by rinsing them gently with cold water and pat dry with a paper towel.
Chop the cleaned roots into small pieces and add 4 cups of boiling water to create a decoction.
Allow the mixture to steep for 10-15 minutes and then strain it through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve.
Consume the resulting liquid in 2-3 doses throughout the day for a total of 6-8 grams of root material daily.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Juniper decoctions helps with urinary calculus because of its potent diuretic and anti-inflammatory properties. Antioxidant and antimicrobial properties: Juniper decoctions have antioxidant and antimicrobial effects, which can help protect against oxidative stress and infection in the urinary tract, reducing the risk of complications from kidney stones.

remedy_recipe: Measure one cup of dried juniperus communis leaves and twigs.
Combine measured herbs with two quarts of boiling water.
Reduce heat and simmer the mixture for 20-30 minutes.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a bowl.
Store the decoction in a clean glass container for up to three days.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice decoctions helps with urinary calculi because of its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and diuretic properties. remedy_recipe: Gather dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots in an amount of 10-15 grams per cup of water.
Boil the root mixture in distilled water for 20 minutes or until the liquid reaches a volume of half.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth into a clean container and discard the solids.
Allow the strained decoction to cool down to room temperature and then refrigerate it at least 30 minutes before use.
Consume the cooled decoction in three divided doses over the course of a day for up to 6 weeks or as directed by a healthcare professional.

Store the prepared decoction in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 24 hours before consumption or freeze it for later use.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat urinary calculus with herbal decoctions is to prepare a combination of herbs such as punarnava, gokshura, and guggulu in equal proportions. Boil 1 tablespoon of this mixture in 2 cups of water until it reduces to half. Strain and drink the decoction three times a day after meals. This helps to dissolve and expel the stone from the urinary tract.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to urinary calculus that are treated with herbal decoctions are various kidney and bladder problems. These include conditions such as nephrolithiasis, urolithiasis, and cystitis, which are characterized by the formation of stones or crystals in the kidneys, ureters, or bladder. Herbal decoctions made from herbs like uva ursi, stinging nettle, and dandelion root have been used traditionally to help dissolve and remove these stone formations, promoting urinary tract health and reducing symptoms such as pain and discomfort.
